summary.intent: Implement task #285: a framework-agnostic GridList core in `ars-components` covering selection, 2D keyboard navigation, typeahead, ARIA/data attrs, agnostic DnD affordances, and loading sentinel. A follow-up question during review also triggered a codebase-wide migration of raw `u32`/`u64` time fields to `core::time::Duration`.
summary.outcome: GridList core was fully implemented, tested (15 unit tests, spec conformance, proptests, 90% branch coverage), and all time-shaped `u32`/`u64` fields across agnostic crates (`ars-core`, `ars-collections`, `ars-forms`, `ars-a11y`, `ars-components`, `ars-test-harness`) were migrated to `Duration`. Specs were synced. A single commit on branch `impl-285` passed `cargo xci --profile fast` and is ready for a PR.

repo: Item `href` must use `SafeUrl` + `sanitize_url()` at output time, not raw `Option<String>`; this is an enforced architecture policy.
GridList core owns selection, highlight, typeahead, stable IDs, and ARIA/data attrs. Live focus, scroll-into-view, measurement, and DOM drag events are adapter-resolved.
TreeView is the canonical reference implementation for agnostic DnD attrs/parts; follow its pattern rather than inventing a new shape.
Agnostic DnD is in scope only when both the GridList spec and existing `ars-collections` DnD APIs define a concrete contract; otherwise spec/code drift must be resolved first.
`cargo xci --profile fast` is the required pre-commit gate. `cargo xcov` enforces a 90% branch coverage minimum on `ars-components`.
`cargo xtask spec validate` validates all 340 spec files and is required before committing.
New snapshots require a `snapshot-reviewed` PR label; `waiting-for-codex-review` must be invoked after each push.
The `Bindable<BTreeSet<Key>>` pattern in core handles controlled selection with parent-lag: pending user selections are preserved while parent props catch up.
`Timer::display_time()` previously returned an unlabelled `(u64, u64, u64, u64)` tuple; migrated to a named `DisplayTime` struct.

code.finding: Previously exposed `set_timeout(delay_ms: u32, ...)` and `now_ms() -> u64`. Both migrated to `Duration`; numeric millisecond conversions now happen only in `ars-dom` at the JS boundary.
Shared `typeahead::State` field was `last_key_time_ms: u64`; `process_char`/`process_char_with_locale` took `u64`. All migrated to `Duration`, removing the need for a GridList-local wrapper type.

JS/CSS boundary: `setTimeout` still requires `i32` ms and CSS animation parsing uses numeric ms internally. The `PlatformEffects` trait now uses `Duration` but the DOM adapter converts to/from numeric at this file boundary only.
`DebouncedAsyncValidator.delay_ms: u32` migrated to `delay: Duration`; the only remaining `AtomicU32` is a cancellation count, not time storage.
`clear_delay_ms: u32` migrated to `clear_delay: Duration` with `DEFAULT_CLEAR_DELAY` as a `Duration` constant.
`DrainAnnouncement { now_ms: u64 }` and `last_announcement_at: Option<u64>` migrated to `Duration`. Numeric `u64` ID counters were intentionally left as-is since they are not time values.
`display_time() -> (u64, u64, u64, u64)` replaced with `-> DisplayTime` struct with named `hours`, `minutes`, `seconds`, `millis` fields.
`delay_ms`, `skip_delay_ms`, keydown timestamps, and `last_close_time: Option<u64>` all migrated to `Duration`/`Option<Duration>`.
Fake virtual-clock fields (`current_ms`, `due_ms`, `interval_ms`) migrated to `Duration`; JS timer handles remain numeric IDs.

workflow: A broad Perl `s///g` regex replacement on wildcard match arms like `Event::TypeaheadSearch(_, _)` produced malformed output `(, Duration::from_millis())`. Mechanical renames across pattern-heavy Rust code should use targeted per-file patches or compile-driven iteration, not regex sweeps.

`cargo insta test --unreferenced=reject -- <filter>` is not safe to use with a test filter: unfiltered snapshots appear unreferenced and cause false failures. Use `--unreferenced=ignore` when running a filtered subset; run the full unfiltered sweep separately.

Plan DnD scope should distinguish adapter DOM work (out of scope) from agnostic state/events/attrs (in scope when spec defines a concrete contract). A blanket 'DnD out of scope' assumption blocked correct implementation.
Running `cargo check` on migrated crates before editing specs surfaces API fallout early and keeps spec sync work grounded in actual compile errors.

When a shared utility type (e.g. `typeahead::State`) exposes a raw numeric field, wrapping it per-component is a short-term band-aid; the real fix is migrating the shared type and updating all consumers in one pass.

friction: `cargo insta test -p ars-components --unreferenced=reject` with any test filter misclassifies unrelated package snapshots as unreferenced; no supported flag scopes the unreferenced scan to a subdirectory.
A broad Perl regex over Rust match arms (`Event::TypeaheadSearch(_, _)`) produced malformed syntax requiring manual repair across five component modules.

The GridList spec had an internal inconsistency: code examples referenced `props.column_count`/`props.row_count` that were not defined in the Props struct, requiring a design decision during planning.
The initial plan's DnD assumption was too coarse; a user clarification mid-session was needed before implementation could start, which would have been avoidable with a more precise spec read upfront.
The `cargo insta` `--glob-filter` flag does not scope the unreferenced scan, making it useless for per-component snapshot validation in a large package.
open_items: Leptos and Dioxus GridList adapters are explicitly deferred; adapter specs exist but no adapter code was written.

Pre-existing unreferenced `utility/highlight` snapshots in `ars-components` will cause `cargo insta test --unreferenced=reject` to fail on any focused run; these need to be cleaned up or accepted separately.
Timer and Tooltip adapter specs now document `Duration` props but the actual Leptos/Dioxus adapter implementations were not updated in this task.
The CSS animation/transition parser in `ars-dom` still works with numeric milliseconds internally (parsing strings like `125ms`); a typed wrapper for CSS time values was not created.

Dioxus and Leptos `tabs_wasm` test wrappers were updated to implement the new `Duration`-based `PlatformEffects` trait signatures, but full wasm integration tests were not run as part of this session.
